input.prompt:
Migrate this .NET 8 library and web API to .NET 9. It was compiled against .NET 9 RC2.
The ICache service is only registered as a non-keyed service.
Identify all breaking changes and provide the exact fixes.

input.initialPrompt:
Migrate this .NET 8 library and web API to .NET 9. It was compiled against .NET 9 RC2.
The ICache service is only registered as a non-keyed service.
Identify all breaking changes and provide the exact fixes.

data.reasoningText: Let me look at the files in the current working directory to understand the codebase.

data.content:
Here are the **4 breaking changes** identified and fixed:

| # | File | Breaking Change | Fix |
|---|------|----------------|-----|
| 1 | `MyLib.csproj` | TFM targets `net8.0` | Updated to `net9.0` |
| 2 | `ApiController.cs` | `[FromKeyedServices("cache")]` throws at runtime in .NET 9 when the service is only registered as non-keyed (keyed/non-keyed DI buckets were split — [breaking change](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/extensions/9.0/keyed-services)) | Removed the attribute; inject `ICache` directly |
| 3 | `ApiController.cs` | `HttpListenerRequest.UserAgent` now returns `null` instead of empty string when header is absent (.NET 9 nullability change) | Added null-conditional (`?.`) with fallback |
| 4 | `StringUtils.cs` | `String.Trim(params ReadOnlySpan<char>)` overload existed in RC2 but was **removed** in GA — passing a `ReadOnlySpan<char>` no longer resolves | Changed to `char[]` so it binds to `String.Trim(params char[])` |
| 5 | `StringUtils.cs` | `[InlineArray]` max length capped at 1 MiB (1,048,576 bytes) in .NET 9 GA; 1,048,577 exceeds the limit | Reduced to 1024 |
